messages.content: These risk write-ups share funnel target nodes, which does NOT make them the same risk — a funnel is coarse, so mechanistically distinct risks (a certification clock, a grid-connection queue, a distributor line-review cycle) can all compile to the same node. PARTITION the cluster into groups of TRUE duplicates: same underlying mechanism, same causal story, such that presenting both to a client would read as repetition. Distinct risks come back as singleton groups (absorbs: []). For a genuine duplicate group, pick the best exemplar (sharpest mechanism, best evidence) as keepId and fold the absorbed write-ups' UNIQUE early-warning indicators into it. Over-merging destroys distinct insights — when in doubt, keep them separate.

content: {"rationale":"Both risks touch the hyperscale/colo segment split, but they describe mechanistically distinct problems. The first is about colo PDU content leaving the category because hyperscale tenants dictate busway/power-shelf specs inside leased halls — a substitution contagion with a null sensitivity band. The second is a double-booking/structure error where the DE×hyperscale beachhead cell is overstated because CE-7 hyperscale is mostly leased colo and the same MW is counted twice across axes. Different causal stories (spec-driven substitution vs. geographic/axis double-counting), different model artifacts (null band vs. rescaled 0.44 overstatement). Keep separate.","groups":[{"keepId":"risk.boundary-substitution.colo-spec-contagion-from-hyperscale-tenants","absorbs":[],"foldedIndicators":[]},{"keepId":"risk.structure-independence.ce-hyperscale-global-split-mirage","absorbs":[],"foldedIndicators":[]}]}
